programme.desc: Alfred har gått vidare från en destruktiv relatio och längtar efter att finna Adonis, sina drömmars man. Men ryktet att Nev och Kamie är i stan för att söka svar leder till den mest explosiva konfrontationen i Catfish historia!

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: Nev och Kamie hjälper en förälskad Navajotjej hitta Zunimannen hon älskar. Han har erkänt sig vara narkotikasmugglare, och ska också ha dött, så nu vill alla ha svar.

programme.title: Caught In The Act: Double Life
programme.desc: Taliahs syster Jameelah har plötsligt blivit tyst. Då hon misstänker en hemlighet som kan skämma ut familjen vänder sig Taliah till Tamar. Men inget kunde förberett Taliah - hennes systers hemlighet är värre än vad hon kunde ha trott.

programme.title: Caught In The Act: Unfaithful
programme.desc: I över fyra år har Gierra dejtat Shay som är klubbpromotor. Gierra vill bilda familj med sin partner, men misstänker att Shays kontakter med andra kvinnor har gett henne foten.

programme.title: Caught In The Act: Unfaithful
programme.desc: I över två år har Nico haft ett seriöst förhållande med sin flickvän Leighann som är frisör. Men nu tror Nico att hon är otrogen och att det är klippt.

programme.title: Ridiculousness
programme.desc: I ett nytt avsnitt av Ridiculousness klär Rob, Steelo och Carly Aquilino upp sig för att ramla i 'Formella fall', ser till att få mer plats i 'Återta utrymme' och inser att det blir mycket städning när man umgås med 'Ministökare'.

programme.title: Ridiculousness
programme.desc: I ett nytt avsnitt av 'Ridiculousness' provar Rob, Steelo och Carly Aquilino några 'Nya steg', några 'Snattade outfits' och snyggar till sig med 'Djärva accessoarer'.

programme.title: Jersey Shore Family Vacation
programme.desc: Gänget förvandlar strandbutiken till en prisutdelning för deras 15-årsjubileum; köttbullarna försöker få Vinnys mamma att må bra; Sammi träffar Rons föräldrar innan hennes embryoöverföring.

programme.title: MTV Unplugged
programme.desc: Denna en timme långa konsert spelades in i april 1996 på Brooklyn Academy of Music i New York. Vi får bland annat höra: "Nutshell

programme.title: Ink Master: Redemption
programme.desc: För första gången kommer mästardukar som kom tvåa tillbaka för att försvara sina tatueringar mot de tatuerare som hindrade dem från att ta titeln. Cleen Rock One, Anthony Michaels, Jime Litwalk och Joey Hamilton återvänder.

programme.title: Ink Master: Redemption
programme.desc: Konstnärerna som blev utslagna först får lite hjälp av gästdomaren Tommy Montoya för att se till att de inte gör samma misstag två gånger på sina upprörda dukar.

programme.title: Ridiculousness
programme.desc: I ett nytt 'Ridiculousness' får Rob, Steelo och Nina Agdal sällskap av Mike 'The Situation' Sorrentino från 'Jersey Shore', halkar till i 'Rutsch, ratsch, boom!' och hittar nya sätt att hetta till det i med 'Eldkastarkillar'.

programme.title: Caught In The Act: Unfaithful
programme.desc: I tre år har Mariah bott med sin pojkvän, pappan till hennes son. Bobby visar ständigt sin kärlek, men en chockerande händelse gör att Mariah undrar om de är fler i relationen.

programme.title: Behind the Music
programme.desc: Boy George minns tillbaka på sin resa från utstött till superstjärna med Culture Club. En tumultartad kärleksaffär, intensivt kändisskap och missbruk förstörde nästan hans karriär. Men 40 år senare är han fortfarande en HBTQ+-ikon. Nytt avsnitt.

programme.title: Jersey Shore Family Vacation
programme.desc: Mike hanterar fortfarande konsekvenserna mellan Angelina och Jenni, och "spelar på båda sidor". Mikes fru, Lauren, är värd för en lanseringsfest för hudvård för "Laurens Skincare". Vinny gör sig redo för sin återkomst till Chippendales-showen i Vegas. Ronnie besöker Mike. Deena och Nicole filmar sitt första avsnitt av "The Meatball Show" och har Jerry Springer som sin första gäst. De har också Aimee Hall från "Floribama Shore" som gäst.

programme.title: Jersey Shore Family Vacation
programme.desc: Gänget drar till San Diego på en ny semesterresa. Angelina och Jenni tjafsar medan Mike uppviglar. Vinny längtar till poolpartyt medan köttbullarna bidar sin tid. Det ser ut att bli ännu en kul semester med gänget!

programme.title: Jersey Shore Family Vacation
programme.sub-title: Det är bra. Jag jobbar här.
programme.desc: Gänget inleder med att åka till Las Vegas. Efter festandet i Syndernas Stad åker de tillbaka österut och firar i Atlantic City innan de slår sig ner vid kusten över sommaren för att återuppleva fornstora dagar.

programme.title: Jersey Shore Family Vacation
programme.desc: Nicole tar med sig en välbekant dräkt för att göra påsken extra speciell, Deenas kvarvarande frustrationer kommer fram under en vinprovning, och alla återvänder till sina rötter med en strandpromenadstur.

programme.title: Celebrity Ex on the Beach
programme.desc: Efter en emotionell dubbeleliminering är Jarred frustrerad och upprörd. Tamara är rädd att historien kan upprepa sig med Jessika Powers, och Callum lägger in en stöt.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Top Italian cooks Antonio Carluccio and Gennaro Contraldo are on a Road Trip around East Anglia. Restaurateurs, writers and old friends the ‘greedy Italians’ will be foraging for antiques in the company of experts David Harper and James Braxton. Watch out for some ‘explosive’ bargaining and an extremely tasty risotto! When he’s not shopping Antonio learns about the brave fisherman of Suffolk who saved mariners’ lives while risking their own. And, Gennaro has a trip to take a peek at a very special bird.

programme.title: Photo Contest
programme.desc: In each episode of Photo Contest, two contestants compete on an imposed theme such as rock'n roll, Japan or architecture. They have one hour to take the best possible photographs before editing them for 30 minutes in a studio. The photographs they have selected are then presented to Agnès Grégoire, editor-in-chief of PHOTO magazine, who will have to choose the best photographer.

programme.title: Artist's Workshop
programme.desc: In the Artist's workshop series, we offer you a privileged meeting with an artist, or the discovery of an artist or an important place for the history of art. Whether they are painters, visual artists, sculptors... These people all have the same passion for art and this series is an opportunity to enter their different universes, to discover what drives them, inspires them and maybe that will give rise to vocations.

programme.title: An hour with
programme.desc: Take an hour break to discover the work of a French painter, Edouard Manet.You will discover his labour with Lola de valence, Olympia, Eel and red mullet.

programme.title: An hour with
programme.desc: Take an hour break to discover the work of a French painter, Paul Cezanne. You will discover his labour with his still-life, mill on the Couleuvre at Pontoise, Young girl with doll.

programme.title: Video Art
programme.desc: Street art, performances, digital art... Because the new forms of Art and their invigorating character, even iconoclastic, have been able to attract a new public until now refractory to go inside the interior of our beautiful museums.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.sub-title: Degas : une passion de la perfection
programme.desc: Exhibition on screen nous emmène à travers les rues de Paris puis au cœur d’une superbe exposition au Fitzwilliam Museum de Cambridge, dont l’impressionnante collection d’œuvres de Degas est la plus représentative de Grande-Bretagne. Ayant bénéficié d’un accès exclusif à une grande variété d’œuvres rares, le film raconte l’histoire fascinante de la quête de perfection de Degas à travers l’expérimentation de nouvelles techniques et les leçons apprises en étudiant les grands maîtres.

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: François Boucher (1703-1770) is the archetypal French Rococo painter. He paints mythological themes tinged with gallantry, for example in the beautiful “Diane resting after her Bath” at the Louvre Museum. His patron was Madame de Pompadour, whose portrait he painted many times. Influenced by the Enlightenment movement, he devoted himself to Chinese themes (“The Chinese Garden”), to pastoral landscape (“Charms of Country Life”) and to a certain sensual Orientalism (“Brown Odalisque”).Enjoy your visit!

programme.title: Architectures
programme.desc: Le Pavillon Allemand de l'exposition universelle de 1929 à Barcelone détient le rapport de la notoriété au m2 bâti. Un toit plat, 8 poteaux métalliques et une dizaine de parois. Comment et pourquoi cette structure minimale en est-elle venue à incarner la modernité du XXème siècle ? En dehors de sa dimension iconique, le Pavillon n'a pas d'autre fonction que d'abriter pour moins d'une heure une unique réception du roi et de la reine d'Espagne, le jour de l'inauguration de l'exposition. Mies van der Rohe donne ici sa propre vision de la nouvelle architecture.

programme.title: Little Sketches
programme.desc: In 64 fun episodes, Gia-Haï Phan teaches you all the sketching techniques you need.

programme.title: Sketchbook
programme.desc: Raphael Federici (street artist), Marine Goodmorning (illustrator), Luca Fiore (cartoonist) and Harry Boudchicha (drawing teacher) reveal their best drawing techniques. Each episode focuses on a specific subject:movement, oil painting, shadows, hair... For the first 10 minutes, each artist gives a theoretical lesson illustrated by sketches. Then they each start working on their creation. Finally, they end with a debrief, comparingand explaining their techniques.

programme.title: Art Quiz
programme.desc: Test your knowledge and your memory in fine arts, architecture, photography, street art and a dozen other topics with our Art Quiz popups !

programme.title: My little Museum
programme.desc: My little Museum is Art History retold through funny anecdotes and backstage trivias by Alicia Flipo. With her wacky demeanor, her inventiveness and her quirky sense of humor, Alicia stages these small Art tales so that once and for all, you could remember the Big stories!

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing’s Zoe Ball and Ian Waite are quickstepping their way across the south West of England in a bid to turn a profit at auction in Hampshire. Experts Margie Cooper and Paul Laidlaw are on hand to guide. Paul shares a secret with Zoe about buying antique hair brushes. Ian, though, offers dancing lessons to dealers to secure a discount. Lordy! Zoe also hears how one village became world famous for lace production while Ian ends up meeting a giant horse with its own boat.

programme.title: Art contest
programme.desc: With just one subject and 45 minutes on the clock, four artists go head-to-head in a public showdown of creativity. In the heart of Le Havre, they must create a unique artwork on the spot, turning a simple theme into a masterpiece. Will they rise to the challenge or crumble under pressure?

programme.title: A stained glass window for 1000 years
programme.desc: To celebrate the thousandth anniversary of its foundation, Strasbourg Cathedral is offering visitors a brand-new stained glass window in the Chapelle Sainte-Catherine.

programme.title: Sketchbook
programme.desc: Raphael Federici (street artist), Marine Goodmorning (illustrator), Luca Fiore (cartoonist) and Harry Boudchicha (drawing teacher) reveal their best drawing techniques. Each episode focuses on a specific subject:movement, oil painting, shadows, hair... For the first 10 minutes, each artist gives a theoretical lesson illustrated by sketches. Then they each start working on their creation. Finally, they end with a debrief, comparingand explaining their techniques.

programme.title: Private Tour
programme.desc: Private Tour takes you to the discovery of the drawing and paper cut exhibition at the Rodin Museum. This exhibition brings together a hundred paper cut-outs and drawings of the artist. These artistic practices have accompanied him throughout his life. Among the works exhibited you will find drawings such as "The Mask of Minos" and many paper cut-outs representing bodies. But it should not be forgotten that Rodin was also a sculptor and you will be able to observe sculptures with Japanese influences such as the "face expressing fury".

programme.title: Private Tour
programme.sub-title: I have the right to have rights
programme.desc: Private Tour takes you to discover the exhibitions on human rights at the Musée de l'Homme in Paris. Nine street artists invaded the walls of the atrium to illustrate articles of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights. It is a unique exhibition because the artists came on site and realized their works in front of the public. Let's discover these engaged and unconventional artistic creations.

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: This famous art gallery in Berlin holds one of the world’s leading collections of European works by Old Masters. Here you can find “The Fountain of Youth” by Cranach the Elder, or the earthy “Netherlandish Proverbs” illustrated by Bruegel. Allow yourself to be tempted by the sensuality of Correggio (“Leda and the Swan”) or of Titian (“Venus with Organist”), by the poetry of “The Glass of Wine”, a genre painting by Vermeer, or by the gracious “Iris or the Dance” by Watteau. Enjoy your visit!

programme.title: Architectures
programme.desc: Le Pavillon Allemand de l'exposition universelle de 1929 à Barcelone détient le rapport de la notoriété au m2 bâti. Un toit plat, 8 poteaux métalliques et une dizaine de parois. Comment et pourquoi cette structure minimale en est-elle venue à incarner la modernité du XXème siècle ? En dehors de sa dimension iconique, le Pavillon n'a pas d'autre fonction que d'abriter pour moins d'une heure une unique réception du roi et de la reine d'Espagne, le jour de l'inauguration de l'exposition. Mies van der Rohe donne ici sa propre vision de la nouvelle architecture.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.sub-title: Canaletto and the Art of Venice
programme.desc: Canaletto et l’art de Venise, un voyage en immersion dans la vie et l’art du célèbre peintre de Venise. Aucun artiste n’a su mieux capturer l’essence et le charme de cette ville que Giovanni Antonio Canal, plus connu sous le nom de Canaletto. La remarquable collection regroupe plus de 200 tableaux, dessins et eaux-fortes nous permet de partir à la découverte du talent artistique de Canaletto, de ses contemporains et de la ville qu’il a su reproduire avec génie. Le film ouvre également les portes de deux résidences royales officielles, Buckingham Palace et le château de Windsor, pour nous en apprendre plus sur l’artiste et sur Joseph Smith, l’homme qui a fait découvrir Canaletto à la Grande-Bretagne.

programme.title: Graffiti World
programme.desc: Graffiti World is a short program dedicated to contemporary urban art around the world. Each episode explores a specific city, highlighting iconic works of street art. Local soundscape accompanies the images for an authentic, commentary-free immersion. The program showcases the creativity and diversity of artistic expression in public spaces. An invitation to discover the richness of graffiti as an art form in its own right.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.sub-title: Le collectionneur danois : de Delacroix à Gauguin
programme.desc: Wilhelm Hansen fut un homme d’affaires visionnaire, qui vécut au Danemark au XIXème siècle. Il fut l’un des rares collectionneurs à s’intéresser aux peintres impressionnistes, à une époque où ils étaient attaqués et dénigrés. Le film nous amène dans la maison d’été de Hansen dans la banlieue de Copenhague et nous fait visiter l’extraordinaire exposition à la Royal Academy de Londres consacrée à sa collection.

programme.title: My little Museum
programme.desc: My little Museum is Art History retold through funny anecdotes and backstage trivias by Alicia Flipo. With her wacky demeanor, her inventiveness and her quirky sense of humor, Alicia stages these small Art tales so that once and for all, you could remember the Big stories!

programme.title: Artist's Workshop
programme.desc: Dans l’Ouest de la banlieue parisienne, nous allons à la découverte de l’atelier de l’artiste Jérôme Zonder. Depuis plus de 10 ans, l’artiste développe un travail virtuose centré sur le dessin. Réalisés au fusain ou à la mine de graphite, ses œuvres sombres et souvent très réalistes suscitent à la fois admiration et effroi. Puissant et complexe, l’art de Jérôme Zonder captive par ses prouesses techniques et questionne notre rapport à la violence.

programme.title: Artist's Workshop
programme.desc: In the Artist's workshop series, we offer you a privileged meeting with an artist, or the discovery of an artist or an important place for the history of art. Whether they are painters, visual artists, sculptors... These people all have the same passion for art and this show is an opportunity to enter their different universes, to discover what drives them, inspires them and maybe that will give rise to vocations.

programme.title: The decisive moment
programme.desc: In this episode, we will meet Lurent Vu, photo reporter. He was born in Nice in 1977, and moved to Paris to study photography. During this period he did an internship at Studio Harcourt, then at Sipa Press, where he still works. He has a taste for portraiture, but also for reportage and journalism.

programme.title: The decisive moment
programme.desc: Lissa Rivera is a Brooklyn-based photographer whose work has received numerous awards and accolades and has been exhibited internationally. Lissa's latest project looks at photography's connection to identity on a personal level, focusing on her domestic partner as her muse. Rivera was chosen as a "Woman to Watch" for the biennial exhibition at the National Museum of Women in the Arts.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing’s Zoe Ball and Ian Waite are quickstepping their way across the south West of England in a bid to turn a profit at auction in Hampshire. Experts Margie Cooper and Paul Laidlaw are on hand to guide. Paul shares a secret with Zoe about buying antique hair brushes. Ian, though, offers dancing lessons to dealers to secure a discount. Lordy! Zoe also hears how one village became world famous for lace production while Ian ends up meeting a giant horse with its own boat.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actor and charmer Nigel Havers and his canny, deal-making chum (and ex-agent) Michael Whitehall explore the West Country. Travelling by Bentley and TVR, they’ll have £400 each and expert advice from Margie Cooper and James Braxton. But what will do best at the auction... Nigel’s old cockle bucket or Michael’s tambourine? Plus there’s a trip to see some Bristol Savages and bit of Georgian elegance with the story of ‘The King of Bath’.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.sub-title: Le curieux monde de Hieronymus Bosch
programme.desc: La ville natale de Bosch aux Pays-Bas ne possède pas de tableaux du peintre et pourtant, pour la grande exposition « Visions of Genius » qui a eu lieu à l’anniversaire de sa mort en 2016, le musée Noordbrabants a réussi à réunir la presque totalité des tableaux et dessins de Bosch. C’est en 2008 que le directeur du musée a commencé sa tournée dans les plus grands musées d’Europe et Etats-Unis pour en persuader les directeurs de prêter au musée Noordbrabants leurs œuvres, en échange d’un travail d’analyse et de recherche sur les tableaux en prêt. Ainsi « Hieronymus Bosch. Visions of Genius » a été l’exposition la plus ambitieuse dédiée au peintre hollandais. Ce documentaire sera une plongée dans l’univers étrange et visionnaire de Hieronymus Bosch.

programme.title: Masterpiece
programme.desc: A l'époque des fastes, Coorte passait des heures à travailler sur ses natures mortes austères composées d'une botte d'asperges, de trois pêches ou d'un bol de fraises. Comment se fait-il qu'il faille huit couleurs différentes pour approcher le blanc des asperges de Coorte ? Et pourquoi sont-elles si transparentes ? La recherche des réponses conduit l'équipe dans une petite église et chez un cultivateur d'asperges dans les provinces néerlandaises, ce qui conduit à une découverte spéciale dans un laboratoire de chimie.

programme.title: Private Tour
programme.desc: Private Tour takes you to discover the exhibition "Architects furniture, 1960-2020" at the Cité de l'Architecture et du Patrimoine in Paris. Why do architects still design furniture today, while designers have taken over the real estate market, lighting...? You will have the answer in this private tour and you will discover furniture of architects such as Nemo.

programme.title: The Edge of Art
programme.desc: Edge of Art captures the relentless drama of a David versus Goliath battle in the art world. Against the gritty backdrop of an American city daring to redefine creativity, this story follows the high-stakes journey to merge the physical world with the digital realm of Web3. At its core, this is a tale of passion, resilience, and ambition, where two art enthusiasts are all-in, risking everything to keep pace with an ever-evolving future. With a daunting $5 million+ price tag hanging over their pursuit, success and failure loom large. The stakes are clear: adapt and soar or falter and lose it all. Each episode plunges viewers into the highs of groundbreaking victories and the lows of crushing setbacks as the rollercoaster of NFTs unfolds and the world of A.I. begins to dominate. Wrestling with each new digital frontier, the protagonists are pushed to their limits. Will they carve out a place in this bold, shifting landscape, or fall victim to the unforgiving edge of redundancy?

programme.title: Museum stories
programme.desc: Before entering the old 19th century mansion which houses the Jean-Jacques Henner museum, it should be noted that it once belonged to another painter, contemporary of Henner: Guillaume Dubufe. The interest of the museum is thus twofold. On the one hand, the visitor takes a trip back in time through the specifics of a mansion built and decorated under the 3rd Republic. On the other, it reveals the work of Jean Jacques Henner, considered at the turn of the 20th century as one of the most important of his time.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.desc: Based on a major exhibition at the Ashmolean Museum in Oxford, Tokyo Stories spans 400 years of incredibly rich art, from Hokusai’s prints to Pop Art posters, via contemporary photography, manga, cinema and street art. The exhibition was a huge success and attracted a significantly younger and more diverse audience than usual. The film draws on the exhibition to take us to Tokyo and explore the city’s art and artistic figures in greater depth. A superbly illustrated and richly detailed documentary, which traces the journey of a metropolis marked by ceaseless cycles of destruction and rebirth throughout its 400-year history, to become one of the most dynamic and fascinating cities in the world today.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.desc: « J’envie les Japonais », écrivait Van Gogh à son frère, Théo. Grâce à l’exposition sur laquelle est basé ce film, « Van Gogh et le Japon », au musée Van Gogh d’Amsterdam, on comprend pourquoi. Bien que Vincent van Gogh n’ait jamais visité le Japon, il reste le pays qui a eu le plus d’influence sur lui et son œuvre. En se rendant dans les galeries d’art japonais à Paris et en créant sa propre image du Japon après des recherches approfondies, des collections d’estampes et de longues discussions avec d’autres artistes, la découverte des œuvres japonaises entraîna la carrière de Van Gogh dans une nouvelle direction très excitante.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.sub-title: Painting the Modern Garden - Monet to Matisse
programme.desc: Passionné d’horticulture, Monet reste sans conteste le maître de la peinture de jardins dans l’histoire de l’art, mais il n’était pas seul. Des artistes tels que Van Gogh, Bonnard, Picasso et Matisse ont utilisé le jardin comme thème fort de leur œuvre. Ces grands artistes, ainsi que bien d’autres noms célèbres, sont rassemblés dans une exposition innovante du Cleveland Museum of Art et de la Royal Gallery of Arts de Londres. De l’exposition elle-même à la beauté des jardins d’artistes comme celui de Monet à Giverny, nous partons en voyage pour découvrir comment le jardin moderne était utilisé pour explorer de nouvelles idées radicales et merveilleuses.

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: Discover or re-discover the masterpieces of the greatest painters of history through our documentary series Within The Frame. From impressionists to surrealists, from Leonard Da Vinci to Paul Cézanne, every episode will immerge you into a virtual tour of a paintings collection. You will see the most impressive paintings ever made and won't miss a single detail.

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: Housing a huge collection of European paintings from the Renaissance at the end of the 18th century, the Alte Pinakothek in Munich brims over with masterpieces. In particular it boasts several paintings by the great Flemish artist, Rubens, such as “The Battle of the Amazons”. Notice as well Durer’s “Paumgartner Altarpiece”, the impressive “Battle of Alexander at Issus” by the painter Altdofer, and also the “Land of Cockaigne” by Pieter Bruegel the Elder. Enjoy your visit!

programme.title: Art Quiz
programme.desc: Test your knowledge and your memory in fine arts, architecture, photography, street art and a dozen other topics with our Art Quiz popups !

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: L'humoriste et animateur Jim Moir et sa femme Nancy Sorrell partent en road trip dans le Kent aujourd'hui.Mieux connu sous le nom de Vic Reeves, Jim apporte sa touche d'humour unique à l'achat d'antiquités. De son côté, Nancy, mannequin et présentatrice télé, sait aussi marchander et elle est très compétitive ! Le couple est accompagné par les experts Charlie Ross et Thomas Plant.Soyez attentif au don surprenant de Jim pour deviner l’âge de presque tout, à Dixon traversant les chutes du Niagara et à une leçon de navigation par longitude.Et surtout, la mystérieuse Mme Parker fera-t-elle son apparition à la vente aux enchères ?

programme.title: Photo Contest
programme.desc: In each episode of Photo Contest, two contestants compete on an imposed theme such as rock'n roll, Japan or architecture. They have one hour to take the best possible photographs before editing them for 30 minutes in a studio. The photographs they have selected are then presented to Agnès Grégoire, editor-in-chief of PHOTO magazine, who will have to choose the best photographer.

programme.title: Artist's Workshop
programme.desc: In the Artist's workshop series, we offer you a privileged meeting with an artist, or the discovery of an artist or an important place for the history of art. Whether they are painters, visual artists, sculptors... These people all have the same passion for art and this show is an opportunity to enter their different universes, to discover what drives them, inspires them and maybe that will give rise to vocations.

programme.title: An hour with
programme.desc: Take an hour break to discover the work of a French painter, Auguste Renoir. You will discover his labour with Girls at the piano, The Bathers, still-life with apples and pears.

programme.title: An hour with
programme.desc: Take an hour break to discover the work of an Italian painter, Raphaël. You will discover his labour with Spozalizio, The virgin and child with St John the Baptist or portrait of Baldassare Castiglione.

programme.title: Video Art
programme.desc: Street art, performances, digital art... Because the new forms of Art and their invigorating character, even iconoclastic, have been able to attract a new public until now refractory to go inside the interior of our beautiful museums.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.desc: Pablo Picasso est l’un des plus grands artistes au monde, et jusqu’à sa mort en 1973, il était le plus prolifique. Comment tout a commencé ? Comment s’est construit Picasso ? Après avoir été trop longtemps ignorées, il est temps de se concentrer sur les jeunes années de l’artiste et son éducation qui lui ont permis d’accomplir l’extraordinaire. Trois villes ont joué un rôle-clé : Malaga, Barcelone et Paris. "Le jeune Picasso" les traverse et explore leur influence sur l’artiste, en analysant des œuvres spécifiques de ses débuts.

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: Museo del Prado in Madrid holds one of the biggest collections of paintings in the world. The “Garden of Delights”, a triptych by Hieronymus Bosch, is one of its jewels. But naturally it is the Spanish School which predominates, with masterpieces by Velasquez and Goya. Look at Velasquez’s famous “Las Meninas” in which the painter portrays himself next to the Infanta and her followers. And admire the sensuous beauty of Goya’s “La Maja Desnuda” or the political force of “El Tres de Mayo”.Enjoy your visit!

programme.title: Architectures
programme.desc: La Pyramide de Djoser, la plus ancienne pyramide égyptienne - 2 600 AJC révèle sans doute les tous premiers pas de ce qu'on appelle l'architecture, comme pratique savante, distincte de la simple construction. Le lieu où s'opère cette coupure est un monument funéraire, où la fonction symbolique prend le pas sur le simple usage. L'architecture à sa naissance s'affirme d'emblée non pas comme fonction mais comme signe. A trente kilomètres au sud du Caire, à l'est la vallée du Nil, Saqqarah fut la nécropole de Memphis, la première capitale de l'ancienne Égypte, dont il ne reste rien sauf cet immense cimetière dominé par les soixante mètres de la pyramide à degrés de Djoser.

programme.title: Little Sketches
programme.desc: In 64 fun episodes, Gia-Haï Phan teaches you all the sketching techniques you need.

programme.title: Sketchbook
programme.desc: Raphael Federici (street artist), Marine Goodmorning (illustrator), Luca Fiore (cartoonist) and Harry Boudchicha (drawing teacher) reveal their best drawing techniques. Each episode focuses on a specific subject:movement, oil painting, shadows, hair... For the first 10 minutes, each artist gives a theoretical lesson illustrated by sketches. Then they each start working on their creation. Finally, they end with a debrief, comparingand explaining their techniques.

programme.title: Art Quiz
programme.desc: Test your knowledge and your memory in fine arts, architecture, photography, street art and a dozen other topics with our Art Quiz popups !

programme.title: My little Museum
programme.desc: My little Museum is Art History retold through funny anecdotes and backstage trivias by Alicia Flipo. With her wacky demeanor, her inventiveness and her quirky sense of humor, Alicia stages these small Art tales so that once and for all, you could remember the Big stories!

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Le rockeur progressif Rick Wakeman et son grand ami Ian Lavender, célèbre pour son rôle de Pike dans Dad’s Army, s’offrent une joyeuse virée sur les routes secondaires du Norfolk. Accompagnés des experts Raj Bisram et David Harper, ces deux chasseurs d’antiquités sillonnent le comté à la recherche de trésors à vendre aux enchères à Nottingham.Rick découvre d’anciens graffitis médiévaux cachés dans la cathédrale de Norwich, tandis qu’Ian fait un détour vers le nord du Norfolk pour admirer la plus grande collection de soldats miniatures au monde.Mais un sage vieil homme japonais aidera-t-il Ian à triompher aux enchères ?

programme.title: Masterpiece
programme.desc: A l'époque des fastes, Coorte passait des heures à travailler sur ses natures mortes austères composées d'une botte d'asperges, de trois pêches ou d'un bol de fraises. Comment se fait-il qu'il faille huit couleurs différentes pour approcher le blanc des asperges de Coorte ? Et pourquoi sont-elles si transparentes ? La recherche des réponses conduit l'équipe dans une petite église et chez un cultivateur d'asperges dans les provinces néerlandaises, ce qui conduit à une découverte spéciale dans un laboratoire de chimie.

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: This famous art gallery in Berlin holds one of the world’s leading collections of European works by Old Masters. Here you can find “The Fountain of Youth” by Cranach the Elder, or the earthy “Netherlandish Proverbs” illustrated by Bruegel. Allow yourself to be tempted by the sensuality of Correggio (“Leda and the Swan”) or of Titian (“Venus with Organist”), by the poetry of “The Glass of Wine”, a genre painting by Vermeer, or by the gracious “Iris or the Dance” by Watteau. Enjoy your visit!

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.sub-title: Rembrandt at the National Gallery, London & Rijksmuseum, Amsterdam
programme.desc: Every Rembrandt exhibition is eagerly awaited, but this exhibition organized by London's National Gallery and Amsterdam's Rijksmuseum was an unprecedented event. The documentary highlights the historic Rembrandt exhibition, while tracing his life. Rembrandt is one of the greatest artists who ever lived, and this deeply moving documentary seeks to explore the truth about the man behind the legend.

programme.title: Sketchbook
programme.desc: Raphael Federici (street artist), Marine Goodmorning (illustrator), Luca Fiore (cartoonist) and Harry Boudchicha (drawing teacher) reveal their best drawing techniques. Each episode focuses on a specific subject:movement, oil painting, shadows, hair... For the first 10 minutes, each artist gives a theoretical lesson illustrated by sketches. Then they each start working on their creation. Finally, they end with a debrief, comparingand explaining their techniques.

programme.title: Private Tour
programme.desc: Private Tour takes you to the discovery of the retrospective "Tutto Ponti" which retraces 60 years of the career of the architect-designer Gio Ponti at the decorative arts museum. This is the first retrospective devoted to this Italian artist. You will discover his ceramic works from the beginning of his career, but also the furniture designs anchored in his poetic and subtle universe.

programme.title: Private Tour
programme.desc: Private Tour takes you to discover the exhibition "Red ! From Jean-Jacques Henner to Sonia Rikiel" at the Jean-Jacques Henner National Museum in Paris. The theme of red hair seems to be a real subject in 19th century painting. During this visit, you will be able to observe paintings by Auguste Renoir, Edgard Maxence but especially Jean-Jacques Henner and Sonia Rikiel.

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: Named Euphrosyne, Thalia and Aglaia, they embody mirth, abundance and splendour. They are the Three Graces, daughters of Jupiter and companions of Venus. A very prized subject as early as the Renaissance, it allowed for a triple representation of the female nude in all its ideal beauty. Observe their grace in the painting by Raphael and the delicacy of their veils in Botticelli’s “La Primavera” or in a magnificent work by Cranach recently acquired by the Louvre Museum. Enjoy your visit!

programme.title: Architectures
programme.desc: La Pyramide de Djoser, la plus ancienne pyramide égyptienne - 2 600 AJC révèle sans doute les tous premiers pas de ce qu'on appelle l'architecture, comme pratique savante, distincte de la simple construction. Le lieu où s'opère cette coupure est un monument funéraire, où la fonction symbolique prend le pas sur le simple usage. L'architecture à sa naissance s'affirme d'emblée non pas comme fonction mais comme signe. A trente kilomètres au sud du Caire, à l'est la vallée du Nil, Saqqarah fut la nécropole de Memphis, la première capitale de l'ancienne Égypte, dont il ne reste rien sauf cet immense cimetière dominé par les soixante mètres de la pyramide à degrés de Djoser.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.sub-title: David Hockney à la Royal Academy of Arts
programme.desc: David Hockney, généralement considéré comme l’un des artistes britanniques les plus populaires, connaît un succès mondial grâce à ses expositions à Londres, New York, Paris et ailleurs qui attirent des millions de spectateurs. Devenu récemment octogénaire, Hockney ne semble pas avoir l’intention de lever le pied ni de perdre son audace légendaire. Ce film révèle des interviews intimes et inédites et se concentre sur deux expositions à succès ayant eu lieu en 2012 et 2016 à la Royal Academy of Arts Londres. Le réalisateur Phil Grabsky a bénéficié d’un accès privilégié pour célébrer sur grand écran ce génie créatif du XXIe siècle.

programme.title: Graffiti World
programme.desc: Graffiti World is a short program dedicated to contemporary urban art around the world. Each episode explores a specific city, highlighting iconic works of street art. Local soundscape accompanies the images for an authentic, commentary-free immersion. The program showcases the creativity and diversity of artistic expression in public spaces. An invitation to discover the richness of graffiti as an art form in its own right.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.desc: Without Camille Pissarro, there is no Impressionist movement. He is rightfully known as the father of Impressionism.It was a dramatic path that Pissarro followed, and throughout it all he wrote extensively to his family. It is through these intimate and revealing letters that this gripping film reveals Pissarro’s life and work.Born in the West Indies, Pissarro found his passion in paint as a young man in Paris, and by the age of 43 had corralled a group of enthusiastic artists into a new collective. Their first show was scorned by the critics, but the group had acquired a new name: the Impressionists. For the next 40 years Pissarro was the driving force behind what has today become the world’s favourite artistic movement.The Ashmolean Museum has a long tradition of excellence. As the UK’s first ever public museum (it opened its doors in May 1683) it houses a wonderfully rich collection, including the remarkable Pissarro archive. With exclusive access to most extensive archive of.

programme.title: My little Museum
programme.desc: My little Museum is Art History retold through funny anecdotes and backstage trivias by Alicia Flipo. With her wacky demeanor, her inventiveness and her quirky sense of humor, Alicia stages these small Art tales so that once and for all, you could remember the Big stories!

programme.title: Artist's Workshop
programme.desc: Joaquin Boz habite et travaille à Buenos Aires. C’est ici, dans la capitale de l’Argentine, qu’il pratique son art à mi-chemin entre abstraction et performance corporelle. Sa peinture est recouverte de couches toujours plus nombreuses. Ces tâches, ces mouvements, ces traits et ces raclures, sont le fruit de la transmission des formes et des couleurs depuis son corps vers la toile dans une performance physique de l’artiste qui puise son inspiration dans le courant expressionniste.

programme.title: Artist's Workshop
programme.desc: In the Artist's workshop series, we offer you a privileged meeting with an artist, or the discovery of an artist or an important place for the history of art. Whether they are painters, visual artists, sculptors... These people all have the same passion for art and this show is an opportunity to enter their different universes, to discover what drives them, inspires them and maybe that will give rise to vocations.

programme.title: The decisive moment
programme.desc: In this episode, we will meet Slam, a portrait photographer. He lives between Paris and New-York, and he is a photographer specialized in beauty, music, fashion, he makes portraits sometimes provocative. He will tell us about his life, his career, and how he became a photographer.

programme.title: The decisive moment
programme.desc: Jady Bates is an internationally known photographer, exhibited at Soho Photo Gallery in New York. She is known for her "darkroom photographer" series, which depicts naked women in black and white with words and/or phrases written on them. She is also the photographer of the band THE SLANTS.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Le rockeur progressif Rick Wakeman et son grand ami Ian Lavender, célèbre pour son rôle de Pike dans Dad’s Army, s’offrent une joyeuse virée sur les routes secondaires du Norfolk. Accompagnés des experts Raj Bisram et David Harper, ces deux chasseurs d’antiquités sillonnent le comté à la recherche de trésors à vendre aux enchères à Nottingham.Rick découvre d’anciens graffitis médiévaux cachés dans la cathédrale de Norwich, tandis qu’Ian fait un détour vers le nord du Norfolk pour admirer la plus grande collection de soldats miniatures au monde.Mais un sage vieil homme japonais aidera-t-il Ian à triompher aux enchères ?

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Les chefs télévisés Aldo Zilli et Ching He-Huang s’affrontent lors de ce Road Trip à travers le sud-est de l’Angleterre, accompagnés des experts Natasha Raskin et Paul Laidlaw.Aldo mise sur une bague en or malgré les conseils des experts, tandis que Ching espère que des antiquités d’origine orientale feront un beau score aux enchères dans le nord de Londres.En chemin, Aldo fait une halte pour rencontrer Jill, une doyenne de 200 ans qui fournissait autrefois la population en farine. De son côté, l’amoureuse des animaux Ching découvre l’histoire d’un homme qui, au XIXe siècle, a risqué le ridicule et la ruine pour protéger le bétail.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.desc: Phil Grabsky, réalisateur récompensé à plusieurs reprises, pose un regard neuf sur celui qui est sans conteste l’artiste le plus aimé au monde en utilisant les mots du peintre lui-même. Moi, Claude Monet révèle de nouvelles facettes de l’homme qui a donné naissance au mouvement impressionniste grâce à un tableau et qui fut certainement l’un des peintres les plus influents et renommés du XIXe et du début du XXe siècle. Tourné à travers l’Europe, sur les lieux où il a peint, Moi, Claude Monet est une exploration filmique inédite et intime de certains des décors cultes les plus appréciés de l’art occidental.

programme.title: Art contest
programme.desc: With just one subject and 45 minutes on the clock, four artists go head-to-head in a public showdown of creativity. In the heart of Le Havre, they must create a unique artwork on the spot, turning a simple theme into a masterpiece. Will they rise to the challenge or crumble under pressure?

programme.title: Le Rendez-vous du dimanche
programme.desc: Every first sunday of the month, Museum TV puts a spotlight on an artist, an artistic current or a famous work of art! In June, follow the footsteps of a master of Flemish art.

programme.title: Private Tour
programme.sub-title: A landscape of excellence, three figures of French architecture
programme.desc: Private Tour takes you to discover the exhibition "A landscape of excellence, three figures of French architecture" at the Cité de l'Architecture & du Patrimoine in Paris. This exhibition offers a journey through contemporary creation. Since the 90s, three figures of French architecture have marked the history of art, Frédéric Borel, Marc Barani and Jean-Marc Ibos and Myrto Vitart. They have shown the excellence of French creations in the world.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.desc: Lucian Freud, l'un des peintres britanniques les plus célèbres de notre époque, est également l'un des rares artistes du XXe siècle à s'être représenté dans un autoportrait avec une telle constance. Ses autoportraits donnent un aperçu fascinant à la fois de sa personnalité et de son évolution en tant que peintre, depuis son premier portrait peint en 1939 jusqu'au dernier. Grâce à des entretiens fascinants avec d'anciens modèles, des amis et d'éminents experts, ce documentaire intensément captivant révèle l'œuvre d'un maître qui représente une étude captivante sur la dynamique du vieillissement et le processus d'autoreprésentation.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.sub-title: Le curieux monde de Hieronymus Bosch
programme.desc: La ville natale de Bosch aux Pays-Bas ne possède pas de tableaux du peintre et pourtant, pour la grande exposition « Visions of Genius » qui a eu lieu à l’anniversaire de sa mort en 2016, le musée Noordbrabants a réussi à réunir la presque totalité des tableaux et dessins de Bosch. C’est en 2008 que le directeur du musée a commencé sa tournée dans les plus grands musées d’Europe et Etats-Unis pour en persuader les directeurs de prêter au musée Noordbrabants leurs œuvres, en échange d’un travail d’analyse et de recherche sur les tableaux en prêt. Ainsi « Hieronymus Bosch. Visions of Genius » a été l’exposition la plus ambitieuse dédiée au peintre hollandais. Ce documentaire sera une plongée dans l’univers étrange et visionnaire de Hieronymus Bosch.

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: Discover or re-discover the masterpieces of the greatest painters of history through our documentary series Within The Frame. From impressionists to surrealists, from Leonard Da Vinci to Paul Cézanne, every episode will immerge you into a virtual tour of a paintings collection. You will see the most impressive paintings ever made and won't miss a single detail.

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: This famous art gallery in Berlin holds one of the world’s leading collections of European works by Old Masters. Here you can find “The Fountain of Youth” by Cranach the Elder, or the earthy “Netherlandish Proverbs” illustrated by Bruegel. Allow yourself to be tempted by the sensuality of Correggio (“Leda and the Swan”) or of Titian (“Venus with Organist”), by the poetry of “The Glass of Wine”, a genre painting by Vermeer, or by the gracious “Iris or the Dance” by Watteau. Enjoy your visit!

programme.title: Art Quiz
programme.desc: Test your knowledge and your memory in fine arts, architecture, photography, street art and a dozen other topics with our Art Quiz popups !

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing’s Zoe Ball and Ian Waite are quickstepping their way across the south West of England in a bid to turn a profit at auction in Hampshire. Experts Margie Cooper and Paul Laidlaw are on hand to guide. Paul shares a secret with Zoe about buying antique hair brushes. Ian, though, offers dancing lessons to dealers to secure a discount. Lordy! Zoe also hears how one village became world famous for lace production while Ian ends up meeting a giant horse with its own boat.

programme.title: Photo Contest
programme.desc: In each episode of Photo Contest, two contestants compete on an imposed theme such as rock'n roll, Japan or architecture. They have one hour to take the best possible photographs before editing them for 30 minutes in a studio. The photographs they have selected are then presented to Agnès Grégoire, editor-in-chief of PHOTO magazine, who will have to choose the best photographer.

programme.title: Artist's Workshop
programme.desc: In the Artist's workshop series, we offer you a privileged meeting with an artist, or the discovery of an artist or an important place for the history of art. Whether they are painters, visual artists, sculptors... These people all have the same passion for art and this series is an opportunity to enter their different universes, to discover what drives them, inspires them and maybe that will give rise to vocations.

programme.title: An hour with
programme.desc: Take an hour break to discover the work of a Franco-Danish painter, Camille Pissarro. You will discover his labour with View from Louveciennes, A path through the fields, Pontoise or Peasant girl making a fire.

programme.title: An hour with
programme.desc: Take an hour break to discover the work of a Prussian painter, Caspar David Friedrich. You will discover his labour with The Wanderer above the mists, Greifswald port or the Lone tree.

programme.title: Video Art
programme.desc: Street art, performances, digital art... Because the new forms of Art and their invigorating character, even iconoclastic, have been able to attract a new public until now refractory to go inside the interior of our beautiful museums.

programme.title: Luana's Kitchen
programme.desc: Avec son amie Myriam, Luana Belmondo prépare deux recettes à base de safran. Au programme : orecchiette (petites pâtes italiennes) avec de la saucisse, et lotte au citron confit avec sa purée d’asperges.

programme.title: Luana's Kitchen
programme.desc: Dans cet épisode, Luana accueille Héloïse Brion, créatrice de "Miss Maggie's Kitchen

programme.title: Health Explored with Mike Wahl
programme.desc: On Jeju Island, home of the legendary Haenyeo divers, we experience one of the most awe-inspiring cultural traditions in the world. These women—many in their 60s, 70s, and 80s—dive deep into cold waters without oxygen tanks, harvesting shellfish and seaweed with incredible skill and stamina.Mike joins them, suiting up to attempt the dive himself. The comparison is humbling: while the Haenyeo descend gracefully and calmly, Mike struggles with breath-holding, buoyancy, and cold exposure. Their mastery is a testament to generations of practice, strength, and community support.After the dive, we cook the day’s catch together, sharing stories and laughter. This episode celebrates resilience, empowerment, and the extraordinary capabilities of women who have shaped the identity of Jeju for centuries.

programme.title: Health Explored with Mike Wahl
programme.desc: Our exploration of ancestry begins in London at the Genealogical Society, where archival records trace Mike’s family back nearly 300 years. With this new information, we travel to Wales, following the path of ancestors through rural landscapes and small villages that still carry the echoes of the past.The journey becomes hands-on as we try sheepdog herding, learning how shepherding has shaped Welsh culture. Next, we experience a unique mental health therapy involving hugging donkeys, discovering how animal connection can reduce stress and promote emotional well-being.With a local forager, we explore edible plants in the Welsh countryside, uncovering how communities have relied on the land for nourishment for generations. The journey concludes in a historic chapel where Mike stands on the exact spot where his ancestor was baptized three centuries earlier—a profound moment of connection across time.

programme.title: Health Explored with Mike Wahl
programme.desc: Our culinary journey through Seoul begins with the smoky, flavour-rich world of Korean BBQ, where sizzling plates and communal dining embody the social heart of Korean culture. From there, we explore one of Seoul’s most iconic fish markets, where we try fresh king crab and live octopus while learning how the country's coastal heritage shaped its food traditions.Next, we visit the bustling herbal medicine market, where shelves overflow with ginseng, roots, mushrooms, and medicinal teas used for centuries in Traditional Korean Medicine. Here, local practitioners explain how balance, Qi, and seasonal eating contribute to long-term health.Our journey ends in Seoul’s thriving coffee scene, tasting artisan brews before joining your friend Chris for a peaceful riverside walk and dessert. This episode reveals that in Korea, food is more than nourishment—it is community, medicine, memory, and identity.

programme.title: Health Explored with Mike Wahl
programme.desc: In a tranquil mountain monastery, Chef-Monk Jeong Kwan invites us into her world of mindful, intentional cooking. Her approach to food is rooted in simplicity, compassion, and a deep respect for nature. Together, we pick vegetables from the temple garden and learn why plant-based, seasonal eating has been central to Korean temple cuisine for generations.A highlight of the episode is learning the art of fermentation—from soy sauce aged in clay pots to rich, umami-packed bean pastes that require months or years to mature. Jeong Kwan explains that fermentation mirrors the human journey: slow, patient, and transformative.Cooking with her becomes a meditation, revealing that food prepared with intention nourishes not only the body but the spirit. This episode challenges us to rethink our relationship with ingredients, time, and the act of eating itself.

programme.title: Health Explored with Mike Wahl
programme.desc: Our season begins in Guatemala with an ascent up the mighty Fuego Volcano, one of the most active volcanoes in the world. As we climb through loose ash and steep ridges, the powerful rumble beneath our feet reveals the dynamic relationship between humans and the natural forces that shape our planet. The climb becomes both a physical challenge and a reminder of the deep connection between environment and well-being.After the volcano, we descend into the world of ancient healing with a traditional Temazcal lodge, where Mayan practitioners guide us through a powerful cleansing ritual using steam, herbs, and ceremony. Inside the dark, humid chamber, the experience becomes a profound demonstration of how culture, community, and spirituality intersect with health.Our journey continues at a coffee plantation, where we learn how altitude, soil, and sustainable farming practices impact not only flavour, but also community health and economic stability. By the end of the episode, we reconnect.

programme.title: Health Explored with Mike Wahl
programme.desc: In this inspiring episode, we join Team Broken Earth on a medical mission in Guatemala, witnessing firsthand the life-changing work taking place in local hospitals. From dawn until night, Canadian and Guatemalan healthcare teams perform surgeries, consult patients, and provide care that is often out of reach for many families.We meet patients of all ages—children awaiting orthopedic procedures, adults seeking mobility restoration, elders who have endured years of pain. Their stories highlight the profound need for global medical partnerships and the impact compassionate care can have on entire communities.Inside the operating rooms, surgical teams work with extraordinary precision, focus, and teamwork. Outside, families wait with hope and gratitude. This episode is a tribute to the power of service, cultural exchange, and the shared humanity that defines global health. Helping others, we learn, is one of the most powerful forms of healing.

programme.title: Massages
programme.desc: Massage studio will show you the best massage techniques in places which will make you dream. Let us take care of you! Lay down, relax and forget all your problems.

programme.title: Yoga
programme.desc: Practice Yoga from the comfort of your home with our experienced teatchers.

programme.title: Yoga
programme.desc: Practice Yoga from the comfort of your home with our experienced teatchers.

programme.title: Audio Advice
programme.desc: Relax and refocus with our thematic audio advices, set against the most beautiful backdrops in the world.

programme.title: My Zen Relaxation
programme.desc: Turn down the lights, kick your shoes back, take a cup of tea... You're on MyZen TV, time to relax.

programme.title: Pilates
programme.desc: Stay in shape with our pilates courses!

programme.title: Namaste Yoga
programme.desc: Séquence joyeuse et élégante, "Grace et Gratitude" ouvre l’avant du corps avec une série d’extensions dynamiques vers l’arrière. Faites rayonner la lumière de votre cœur en apprenant à respirer avec ampleur. Cette pratique encourage une action guidée par des intentions d’amour, de pardon et de gratitude.

programme.title: Spiritual Sports
programme.desc: Nurture the body AND the mind with Spiritual Sports.

programme.title: Home training
programme.desc: Dans cet épisode de Fais ton Sport à la Maison, place au cardio training ! Sabrina et Guigui vous proposent une séance dynamique avec des déplacements, des sauts et des torsions pour faire monter le rythme cardiaque tout en s’amusant. L’échauffement est précis et complet pour mobiliser toutes les articulations. L’ambiance est complice, pleine de blagues, mais les exercices sont efficaces ! Préparez votre bouteille d’eau, poussez les meubles, et laissez-vous porter par l’énergie de la team.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook
programme.desc: Julie prend la direction de la Bourgogne pour découvrir des races anciennes de volaille réintroduites en France grâce au travail de l’éleveur Fred Ménager. Nous nous rapprocherons ensuite de l’Auvergne pour découvrir comment est attribué le fameux Label Rouge et le rôle des organismes certificateurs … car il est important de savoir ce qui atterri dans nos assiettes. Julie se rendra aussi dans la Drôme pour cuisiner de la pintade avant de se diriger vers le Périgord pour préparer un civet de dindon. Installée dans sa petite Micheline, Julie remontera ensuite vers le centre pour découvrir un étonnant fabricant de mouche qui utilise des plumes de coqs !

programme.title: Yoga
programme.desc: Practice Yoga from the comfort of your home with our experienced teatchers.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook
programme.desc: Si l’été est la saison où les légumes abondent au potager, il serait dommage de ne pas savourer ceux que les saisons plus fraîches nous offrent : potages, tourtes, potées ou gratins mettent à l’honneur des légumes de saisons, idéals pour se réchauffer. Pour débuter ce nouveau voyage, un légume qui trônait déjà sur les tables de la Grèce Antique : le salsifis que Julie cuisinera avec une drôle de tourtière…L’hiver c’est aussi la saison du chou alors direction la Champagne pour y apprendre la recette de la potée locale qui devrait remplir le ventre d’un grand nombre de convives.Ce petit tour de France nous emmènera aussi à la découverte d’orfèvres du maraîchage qui sculptent légumes et fruits d’hiver, et vous présentera différentes techniques et méthodes pour sublimer vos potagers.

programme.title: Namaste Yoga
programme.sub-title: Strengthen and Surrender: Lower Body Balance for Hips and Hamstrings
programme.desc: This moving meditation leads you through a series of postures that will strengthen the hips and hamstrings. Find balance between ease and effort while bringing a sense of curiosity to your experience of each posture.

programme.title: Namaste Yoga
programme.sub-title: Anahata Flow: Open Hips, Shoulders and Heart
programme.desc: A heart-centered practice, Anahata Flow allows the body to seek the freedom of the sky while unifying with the breath.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook
programme.desc: If you're a nature enthusiast and a fan of outdoor cooking, the upcoming season is perfect for barbecue enthusiasts! Our journey will take us to Aube, nestled in the heart of the Brévonnes forest, and then to the Massif des Bauges, where grilling and snow create a unique combination. Afterward, we'll explore the Libournais region, where we'll uncover cooking techniques that make use of vine shoots, which are plentiful in the area. Just a short distance from the vineyards, we'll arrive at another corner of Gironde, the Blaye region, where the cooking method remains consistent but features a distinctly local ingredient: eel.

programme.title: Deco Masters
programme.desc: This week, Kenelm, Aurélie, Virginie and Fred are the candidates for the title of Ace of the Week. But before they go head-to-head on their building sites, our budding decorators have a meeting in the Brussels workshop. It's a chance for them to gauge their opponents. On this first day of competition, Kenelm takes to the stage. At just 24, this Brussels native is the youngest decorator of the week. With a degree in architecture, he has developed a style all his own. An adept of minimalism and respectful of materials above all else, he hides another secret weapon...

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook
programme.desc: Today, Julie takes us down a different kind of yellow-brick road, one that tastes of childhood and gourmet delights: the road of sweets. France may be famous for its gastronomy, but it's not to be outdone when it comes to pastries and confectionery - quite the opposite. “Bêtises” from Cambrai, "Berlingots” from Carpentras, "Calissons” from Aix-en-provence, every region in France has its own sweet or confectionery.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook
programme.desc: Julie takes us on a journey of spices and aromatic plants. Spices are a call to travel, to faraway lands and to the unknown. On her journey, Julie will introduce us to the famous black pepper.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook
programme.desc: Direction le Loir-et-Cher pour une chasse à courre encadrée par les équipes de l’ONCFS. Prenons ensuite la route des Vosges pour cuisiner aux côtés de Mauricette une savoureuse gigue de cerf. Allons à la rencontre de Pierre en Côte d’Or où il fabrique des trompes de chasse, un outil indispensable à la vénerie. Julie retrouvera aussi deux chasseurs girondins pour une après-midi de chasse à la tonne dès plus originale avant de rejoindre Joël en Aquitaine pour une autre recette de gibier à plume. Nous parlerons ensuite avec Laurent du métier de piqueux : ce dresseur de chiens destinés à la vénerie. Cette fois-ci ce sera Frédéric Robert qui nous concoctera une recette de lièvre frotté au cacao et cuit dans un sarcophage d’argile.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook
programme.desc: Julie is offering you a trip to the south of France. Between the Rhone Valley and the Mediterranean, let's discover a fruit that is harvested in November: the olive.

programme.title: Luana's Kitchen
programme.desc: Dans cet épisode, le potiron est à l'honneur. Luana Belmondo cuisine un velouté de potiron aux épices avec des sablés salés au parmesan. Et pour la suite, elle prépare des gnocchis de potiron avec une sauce au fromage onctueuse.

programme.title: Luana's Kitchen
programme.desc: Dans cet épisode, Luana Belmondo invite son amie Eleonora Galasso pour une pizza party ! Elles vont d'abord préparer une pâte maison. Ensuite, elles cuisineront une capriciosa avec des anchois, des câpres et du jambon, ainsi qu'une pizza fritta avec de l’origan et de la sauce tomate.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook
programme.desc: It is a mushroom that we are going to focus on today. A mushroom, or a black diamond, some would say: the truffle.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook
programme.desc: Aujourd’hui, les plus gourmands ne devraient pas être déçus puisque c’est le chocolat qui est mis à l’honneur. Découvert par les mayas il y 2500 ans et plus largement ensuite par les Aztèques, le chocolat fait partie aujourd’hui des produits préférés des français. Et qu’on ne s’y trompe pas : au même titre que la cuisine a ses grands chef, le chocolat a aussi ses grands chocolatiers. Coté recettes, Julie fera découvrir un gâteau qu’on ne fait qu’à Saint Émilien et qui associe le chocolat au macaron, avant de rejoindre le Pays Basque pour un dessert qui ne manque pas de piment. Nous grimperons ensuite en montagne à la rencontre d’une cuisinière d’origine suisse qui fait des pains au chocolat à sa façon. Enfin, vous verrez que le chocolat nous réserve de nombreuses surprises : il possède de nombreuses vertus et s’utilise aussi pour le bien être.

programme.title: Home training
programme.desc: In this episode of Home Training, the team guides you through a targeted upper body session. With a thoughtful warm-up, precise moves, and tons of good vibes, this workout focuses on arms, shoulders, and core. The coaches bring humor, energy, and support for a safe and effective effort. Every exercise is clearly explained and adaptable so everyone can follow along at their own pace. A perfect routine to build strength while having fun!

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook
programme.desc: Eating flowers might appear unconventional, but we often consume them without even realizing it. Consider artichokes, for instance : they are essentially large, unopened flowers. Capers are the preserved buds of flowers soaked in vinegar, and cloves are dried flower buds. We also find courgette flowers, saffron pistils, orange blossom water used to flavor pastries, and the violet sweets that evoke memories of our childhood. In addition to adding aesthetic appeal and providing a dose of vitamins to our plates, flowers elevate the flavors of a dish and can even serve as a vegetable substitute.

programme.title: Namaste Yoga
programme.sub-title: Release and Restore: A Sequence for Your Spine
programme.desc: Release and Restore is a sequence that brings you full circle through a series of poses that create space for reflection. You will release tension in your spine while attuning to the changes even the smallest actions create. What change will you make today?

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook
programme.desc: Cap au Nord pour découvrir une ville française absolument incontournable : Lille. Cette ville possède un patrimoine architectural exceptionnel, marchande dès le Moyen Age, citadelle sous Louis XIV, industrielle au XIXe...elle s'agrandit au cours des siècles. Julie ira à la rencontre de ceux qui participent à la renommée de la ville et de la région, et à travers son patrimoine culinaire avec au menu de ce voyage : la recette du coq à la bière, celle du hochepot, et sur une note sucrée, une crème brûlée à la chicorée. .

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook
programme.desc: Julie nous donne rendez-vous à Bordeaux après avoir arpenté le pays de Blaye, visité deux fois le bassin et parcouru les terres du Libournais à deux reprises, il fallait bien s’arrêter un jour dans la capitale du Bordelais ! N’y-a-t-il pas meilleure saison que le mois de septembre pour découvrir la perle d’Aquitaine, plus souvent appelée « Cité du vin » ? Julie donne rendez-vous à ces habitants pour découvrir les traditions mais aussi les projets plus récents de cette ville chargée d’Histoire et de gastronomie.

programme.title: Luana's Kitchen
programme.desc: Luana Belmondo offers a recipe for celeriac risotto with citrus powder. She will also prepare black rice with spring vegetables. A real treat for the taste buds!

programme.title: Luana's Kitchen
programme.desc: Discover an Italian culinary staple: ravioli. Luana Belmondo, accompanied by her son Alessandro, makes her recipe for ricotta arugula ravioli with artichoke and almond sauce. The duo also prepare choco ravioli stuffed with pistachio custard and strawberry coulis.

programme.title: Big Little Footprints
programme.desc: We travel to an island resort in Fiji known for it’s daring shark encounters! To hear and see first hand the amazing conservation work they’re doing. Along the way we meet the local school kids and learn about how they’re helping protect the reef.

programme.title: Health Explored with Mike Wahl
programme.desc: Our exploration of ancestry begins in London at the Genealogical Society, where archival records trace Mike’s family back nearly 300 years. With this new information, we travel to Wales, following the path of ancestors through rural landscapes and small villages that still carry the echoes of the past.The journey becomes hands-on as we try sheepdog herding, learning how shepherding has shaped Welsh culture. Next, we experience a unique mental health therapy involving hugging donkeys, discovering how animal connection can reduce stress and promote emotional well-being.With a local forager, we explore edible plants in the Welsh countryside, uncovering how communities have relied on the land for nourishment for generations. The journey concludes in a historic chapel where Mike stands on the exact spot where his ancestor was baptized three centuries earlier—a profound moment of connection across time.

programme.title: Big Little Footprints
programme.desc: To educate our children about the profound impact of travel we travel to Miami, Florida. We'll learn about the area's colorful history, taste test its cuisine, learn Latin dancing and experience an epic adventure through the everglades - if we can make it out of alligator infested waters first!

programme.title: Health Explored with Mike Wahl
programme.desc: Our culinary journey through Seoul begins with the smoky, flavour-rich world of Korean BBQ, where sizzling plates and communal dining embody the social heart of Korean culture. From there, we explore one of Seoul’s most iconic fish markets, where we try fresh king crab and live octopus while learning how the country's coastal heritage shaped its food traditions.Next, we visit the bustling herbal medicine market, where shelves overflow with ginseng, roots, mushrooms, and medicinal teas used for centuries in Traditional Korean Medicine. Here, local practitioners explain how balance, Qi, and seasonal eating contribute to long-term health.Our journey ends in Seoul’s thriving coffee scene, tasting artisan brews before joining your friend Chris for a peaceful riverside walk and dessert. This episode reveals that in Korea, food is more than nourishment—it is community, medicine, memory, and identity.

programme.title: Big Little Footprints
programme.desc: Join us on an epic adventure to a remote private island in Belize. As we spend our days catching lobsters, diving the worldclass reef and enjoying coconuts straight from the tree. We also learn about how much our tourism dollars really count.

programme.title: Health Explored with Mike Wahl
programme.desc: In a tranquil mountain monastery, Chef-Monk Jeong Kwan invites us into her world of mindful, intentional cooking. Her approach to food is rooted in simplicity, compassion, and a deep respect for nature. Together, we pick vegetables from the temple garden and learn why plant-based, seasonal eating has been central to Korean temple cuisine for generations.A highlight of the episode is learning the art of fermentation—from soy sauce aged in clay pots to rich, umami-packed bean pastes that require months or years to mature. Jeong Kwan explains that fermentation mirrors the human journey: slow, patient, and transformative.Cooking with her becomes a meditation, revealing that food prepared with intention nourishes not only the body but the spirit. This episode challenges us to rethink our relationship with ingredients, time, and the act of eating itself.

programme.title: Big Little Footprints
programme.sub-title: Eco-Explore Your Backyard - California Coast To Santa Barbara
programme.desc: In this episode we remind viewers just how much you can find when exploring your own backyard by road tripping the California Coast to Santa Barbara! Swapping TV time to chats around the campfire and an exciting encounter with sea lions.

programme.title: Health Explored with Mike Wahl
programme.desc: Our season begins in Guatemala with an ascent up the mighty Fuego Volcano, one of the most active volcanoes in the world. As we climb through loose ash and steep ridges, the powerful rumble beneath our feet reveals the dynamic relationship between humans and the natural forces that shape our planet. The climb becomes both a physical challenge and a reminder of the deep connection between environment and well-being.After the volcano, we descend into the world of ancient healing with a traditional Temazcal lodge, where Mayan practitioners guide us through a powerful cleansing ritual using steam, herbs, and ceremony. Inside the dark, humid chamber, the experience becomes a profound demonstration of how culture, community, and spirituality intersect with health.Our journey continues at a coffee plantation, where we learn how altitude, soil, and sustainable farming practices impact not only flavour, but also community health and economic stability. By the end of the episode, we reconnect.

programme.title: Massages
programme.desc: Massage studio will show you the best massage techniques in places which will make you dream. Let us take care of you! Lay down, relax and forget all your problems.

programme.title: Health Explored with Mike Wahl
programme.desc: In this inspiring episode, we join Team Broken Earth on a medical mission in Guatemala, witnessing firsthand the life-changing work taking place in local hospitals. From dawn until night, Canadian and Guatemalan healthcare teams perform surgeries, consult patients, and provide care that is often out of reach for many families.We meet patients of all ages—children awaiting orthopedic procedures, adults seeking mobility restoration, elders who have endured years of pain. Their stories highlight the profound need for global medical partnerships and the impact compassionate care can have on entire communities.Inside the operating rooms, surgical teams work with extraordinary precision, focus, and teamwork. Outside, families wait with hope and gratitude. This episode is a tribute to the power of service, cultural exchange, and the shared humanity that defines global health. Helping others, we learn, is one of the most powerful forms of healing.

programme.title: Massages
programme.desc: Massage studio will show you the best massage techniques in places which will make you dream. Let us take care of you! Lay down, relax and forget all your problems.

programme.desc: Are you ready to fly? Reflect and Connect will engage your whole body with a series of poses that first calm and center, and then awaken and inspire. Build energy and a sense of security before channeling your curiosity and testing your limits.

programme.title: Home training
programme.desc: In this episode of Home Training, Guillaume and Sabrina guide you through a complete lower-body strengthening session. After a warm-up, they move on to targeted exercises for the thighs, abs, and glutes. Squats, lunges, and lateral raises are on the program to build strength and tone your body.

programme.desc: Stay in shape with our pilates courses!

programme.title: Namaste Yoga

programme.desc: Connect with the essence of your being with Reconnect and Refine. This exhilarating sequence encourages you to find comfort in the present moment, and let go of preconceived expectations or agendas. It is not about where you are going, but where you are.
